task:
# Relay stage 6: Implement
Task: llm-models-configurable-in-the-ui
Working directory: /Users/nicholaswestby/Dev/visual-relay/

## Task input
# LLM Models Configurable in the UI

Let the user pick which concrete model each tier uses, from a dropdown per tier in Settings → "Live Tiers". Today that panel is read-only: it shows the auto-resolved model per tier but offers no way to change it. Add a curated dropdown (≤6 options per tier, drawn only from the five provider APIs already in use — Hugging Face, DeepSeek, Moonshot, Anthropic, OpenAI), persist the choice per-repo, and wire it through so the generated backend config actually honors it.

## Current state (researched)

- **Tiers** live in `BackendConfigGenerator.Chains` (`src/VisualRelay.Core/Configuration/BackendConfigGenerator.cs`), a `Dictionary<string, List<(string Model, string RequiredKey)>>` keyed by `cheap`, `balanced`, `frontier`, `vision`, `claude`, `fallback`.
- **Real models** are the `model_name` entries in `tools/backend/litellm-config.yaml` `model_list`: `glm-5.2`(HF), `kimi-k2`(Moonshot), `deepseek-v4-pro`/`deepseek-v4-flash`(DeepSeek), `hf-qwen3-coder-next`(HF), `hf-qwen3-vl-235b`/`hf-qwen3-vl-30b`(HF), `claude-opus-1m`/`claude-sonnet`(Anthropic), `gpt-5`(OpenAI). All five providers are represented; `gpt-5` is defined and priced (`RelayPricing.Default["gpt-5"]`) but currently sits in no tier chain.
- **Resolution** is automatic: `BackendConfigGenerator.ResolveTiers(ISet<string> presentKeys)` picks each tier's first chain candidate whose key is present; `GetTierRows(presentKeys)` and `Generate(presentKeys, templatePath)` take **only** `presentKeys` today — there is no override path.
- **UI**: `SettingsPanel.axaml` "Live Tiers" `ItemsControl` binds `LitTierRows` with a `DataTemplate x:DataType="cfg:BackendConfigGenerator+TierConfigRow"` rendering `Tier`/`Model`/`ProviderName`/`KeyPresent` as static `TextBlock`s (no input). `TierConfigRow` is an immutable `record`.
- **VM**: `MainWindowViewModel.Keys.cs` — `LitTierRows` is `ObservableCollection<BackendConfigGenerator.TierConfigRow>`; `RefreshLitTiers()` calls `GetTierRows` (+ `Generate` for the summary). `RefreshKeyStatesAsync()` rebuilds it.
- **Effect path**: `BackendLifecycle.LaunchProxyAsync` → `BackendConfigStep.ResolveAsync(_paths, _options.RepoRoot, …)` → `BackendConfigStep.Generate` reads env keys and calls `BackendConfigGenerator.Generate(present, template)`, writing `.relay-scratch/litellm-config.generated.yaml`. Applied at backend start.
- **Persistence pattern**: `RelayConfigWriter.UpsertX` does read-modify-write on `.relay/config.json` preserving all other keys (e.g. `UpsertCommitProofArtifacts`); `RelayConfigLoader.TryLoadAsync` reads into `RelayConfig`; the VM's `OnXChanged` calls the writer. `RelayConfig` already has `TierProfiles` (tier→profile name).

## What to build (TDD-first)

1. **Curated selectable lists.** Add to `BackendConfigGenerator` a `SelectableModels` map: tier → ordered list of `model_name`s (≤6 each, only the five providers, only real `model_list` models). Use exactly these (defaults match today's auto-resolution):
   - `frontier`: `glm-5.2`, `kimi-k2`, `deepseek-v4-pro`, `claude-opus-1m`, `gpt-5`, `hf-qwen3-coder-next`
   - `balanced`: `deepseek-v4-pro`, `kimi-k2`, `deepseek-v4-flash`, `gpt-5`, `hf-qwen3-coder-next`, `claude-sonnet`
   - `cheap`: `deepseek-v4-flash`, `deepseek-v4-pro`, `hf-qwen3-coder-next`, `gpt-5`
   - `vision`: `hf-qwen3-vl-235b`, `hf-qwen3-vl-30b`, `kimi-k2`
   - `claude`: `claude-opus-1m`, `claude-sonnet`
   - `fallback`: `hf-qwen3-coder-next` — **not user-editable** (it is the always-available HF floor; keep it display-only).
2. **Override-aware resolution.** Thread an optional `IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string>? overrides` through `BackendConfigGenerator.ResolveTiers` / `GetTierRows` / `Generate`. Semantics, final: when a tier has an override **and** that model's required key is present, the override is the alias and the fallback chain is the remaining survivors (still terminating in `fallback` for non-`claude` tiers). When the override's key is absent, **ignore it** and auto-resolve — preserving the existing dead-primary-elimination guarantee so a missing key never breaks boot. `GetTierRows` rows additionally expose `SelectableModels` and `IsEditable` (`false` only for `fallback`).
3. **Persist + load.** Add `RelayConfig.TierModelOverrides` (`IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string>?`, default null). `RelayConfigLoader.TryLoadAsync` reads a new `tierModelOverrides` object from `.relay/config.json`, dropping any entry whose value is not in that tier's `SelectableModels`. Add `RelayConfigWriter.UpsertTierModelOverrides(rootPath, IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string>)` mirroring `UpsertCommitProofArtifacts` (read-modify-write, preserve other keys).
4. **VM wiring** (`MainWindowViewModel.Keys.cs`): replace the bare `TierConfigRow` binding with a per-tier observable row carrying two-way `SelectedModel`, `SelectableModels`, `IsEditable`, `ProviderName`, `KeyPresent`. Hydrate `SelectedModel` from the loaded `RelayConfig.TierModelOverrides` (falling back to the auto-resolved model) on `LoadInitialAsync`/`OpenSettingsAsync`. On `SelectedModel` change: `RelayConfigWriter.UpsertTierModelOverrides` then `RefreshLitTiers`.
5. **UI** (`SettingsPanel.axaml`): in the "Live Tiers" `DataTemplate`, replace the static `Model` `TextBlock` with a `ComboBox` (`ItemsSource` = `SelectableModels`, `SelectedItem` two-way = `SelectedModel`, `IsEnabled` = `IsEditable`). Keep the tier name + provider/key dot as today.
6. **Effect path**: `BackendConfigStep.Generate` loads `RelayConfig.TierModelOverrides` from the repo root (or receives them from `BackendLifecycle.LaunchProxyAsync`, which already has `_options.RepoRoot`) and passes them to `BackendConfigGenerator.Generate`, so the generated `litellm-config.generated.yaml` honors overrides at the next backend (re)start. State this "applies on next backend start" in the UI (a short note under Live Tiers).

**Test order (write red first):** `BackendConfigGeneratorTests` — override wins when key present; override ignored when key absent; chain still terminates in `fallback`; `SelectableModels` per-tier shape + ≤6; `GetTierRows` exposes `IsEditable`/`SelectableModels`. `RelayConfigLoader`/`RelayConfigWriter` — round-trip of `tierModelOverrides`, invalid entries dropped, other keys preserved. `SettingsPanelUiTests` (`[AvaloniaFact]`) — a `ComboBox` per editable tier with the right options; changing `SelectedModel` writes `tierModelOverrides` to `.relay/config.json` and survives reload; `fallback` row's combo is disabled.

## Done when

- Each editable tier (`cheap`, `balanced`, `frontier`, `vision`, `claude`) shows a dropdown with ≤6 options, all from the five in-use providers' real `model_list` models; `fallback` is display-only.
- Selecting a model persists `tierModelOverrides` to `.relay/config.json`, survives a config reload, and is reflected as the tier's alias in `BackendConfigGenerator.Generate` output **when its provider key is present**.
- An override whose key is absent is ignored at resolution (tier auto-resolves; boot never breaks).
- The fallback chain for every non-`claude` tier still terminates in `fallback`.
- `./visual-relay check` is green (file-size guard, format, build, tests, screenshot).

## Guardrails

- Run the full gate: `./visual-relay check`. Keep C# and Avalonia XAML source ≤300 lines — `BackendConfigGenerator.cs` (~269) and `SettingsPanel.axaml` (~295) are near the limit; split into partials (e.g. `BackendConfigGenerator.Selectable.cs`) or a small sub-control rather than bloating them.
- Core tests use xUnit v3 `[Fact]`; UI tests use `[AvaloniaFact]` + `[Collection("Headless")]`; `HeadlessUnitTestSession` is banned. **`BackendConfigGeneratorTests` is an oversized family**: any new `[Fact]` added there requires bumping `const int baseline = 159` in `SplitGuardVerificationTests.FactCount_AcrossOversizedFiles_MatchesBaseline` (UI `[AvaloniaFact]`s are not counted).
- Conventional Commits directly on `main` (no PRs); the `commit-msg` hook enforces subject rules. Minimal diffs — change only what this task needs; do not reformat unrelated code.
- No new provider APIs or keys; reuse the existing five. No pricing changes (the run report's `model` is the tier/profile name, not the alias target, so pricing is unaffected and out of scope).

src/VisualRelay.Core/Configuration/RelayConfigLoader.TierModelOverrides.cs
tests/VisualRelay.Tests/BackendConfigGeneratorTests.Selectable.cs

## Prior stages
## Stage 1 - Ideate

{
  "summary": "Add LLM model selection dropdowns per tier in Settings → Live Tiers. The feature spans a static SelectableModels map (partial class), override-aware resolution threading, persistence via tierModelOverrides in .relay/config.json, a richer ViewModel row, ComboBox UI, and effect-path wiring. Key constraints: 300-line file limits, 159-fact baseline in BackendConfigGeneratorTests, no new providers, TDD-first.",
  "options": [
    "Option A (recommended): Static SelectableModels dictionary in a new BackendConfigGenerator.Selectable.cs partial; thread optional overrides through existing methods; minimal VM/UI changes; simplest diff and best fit for ≤6 fixed options.",
    "Option B: Store selectable model lists in .relay/config.json alongside TierProfiles; fully data-driven but over-engineered for the fixed set and adds config migration complexity.",
    "Option C: Extract a TierModelSelector service class for selection/persistence logic; cleaner separation but heavier refactor than needed for this scope."
  ]
}

## Stage 2 - Research

Baseline fact count=159, any new [Fact] in BackendConfigGeneratorTests requires bumping it.", "constraints": ["BackendConfigGenerator.cs (269 lines) and SettingsPanel.axaml (295 lines) are near the 300-line file-size guard — need partial class split or sub-control extraction", "Baseline [Fact] count in SplitGuardVerificationTests.FactCount_AcrossOversizedFiles_MatchesBaseline is 159; every [Fact] added to BackendConfigGeneratorTests requires bumping it", "RelayConfigLoader.cs (297 lines) near the 300 limit — tierModelOverrides loading must be minimal", "No new provider APIs or keys — reuse HL_TOKEN, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, MOONSHOT_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY only", "No pricing changes — the run report model is the tier/profile name, not the alias target", "UI tests use [AvaloniaFact] + [Collection(\"Headless\")] — these are NOT counted in the 159 baseline", "Conventional Commits directly on main; commit-msg hook enforces subject rules", "The gpt-5 model is defined and priced but sits in no tier chain today — must be added to selectable lists", "fallback tier must remain display-only (IsEditable=false) as per task spec"]}

The codebase is in a known-clean starting state: no SelectableModels map, no TierModelOverrides field on RelayConfig, no override parameter on ResolveTiers/GetTierRows/Generate, and no UpsertTierModelOverrides writer method exist. The effect path (BackendConfigStep.Generate → BackendConfigGenerator.Generate) reads env keys only with no override path. Three files are at or near the 300-line guard limit: BackendConfigGenerator.cs (269 lines), SettingsPanel.axaml (295 lines), and RelayConfigLoader.cs (297 lines), requiring partial-class splits or sub-control extraction for new code. The BackendConfigGeneratorTests oversized family has 20 [Fact]s (9 main + 7 PerModelTimeout + 3 KimiK2_7Upstream + 1 AliasConsistency), and the SplitGuardVerificationTests baseline is 159 total across all oversized families; each new [Fact] in this family requires bumping the constant. gpt-5 (OpenAI) exists in model_list and RelayPricing but is absent from all tier Chains — it must be added to the selectable lists for frontier/balanced/cheap. The BoolNotConverter referenced in the XAML (line 270) is in the controls namespace as controls:BoolNotConverter.Instance — this pattern should be followed for consistency.",

## Stage 4 - Plan

{
  "plan": "## LLM Models Configurable in the UI — Implementation Plan\n\n### Overview\nAdd a per-tier model-selection dropdown (ComboBox) to Settings → \"Live Tiers\", persist the choice in `.relay/config.json` under `tierModelOverrides`, and thread overrides through `BackendConfigGenerator` so the generated litellm config honors them. `fallback` tier stays read-only.\n\n---\n\n### Step 1: Test-first — BackendConfigGeneratorTests.Selectable.cs (new, red)\n\nCreate `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/BackendConfigGeneratorTests.Selectable.cs` with 4 new `[Fact]`s:\n\n1. **`Override_WinsWhenKeyPresent`** — Given overrides `{ \"cheap\": \"deepseek-v4-pro\" }` with `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` present → the cheap tier alias is `deepseek-v4-pro` and its fallback chain terminates in `fallback`.\n2. **`Override_IgnoredWhenKeyAbsent`** — Override model's required key absent (e.g., `gpt-5` without `OPENAI_API_KEY`) → override is silently ignored, tier auto-resolves via normal chain.\n3. **`SelectableModels_PerTierShapeAndCapped`** — Each tier's list is ≤6 entries, all entries come from the real `model_list` names, only five providers.\n4. **`GetTierRows_ExposesIsEditableAndSelectableModels`** — `IsEditable` is `true` for all tiers except `fallback` (which is `false`); `SelectableModels` populated for every tier including fallback.\n\n**Bump baseline:** In `SplitGuardVerificationTests.FactCount_AcrossOversizedFiles_MatchesBaseline`, change `const int baseline = 159` → `const int baseline = 163`.\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: BackendConfigGenerator.Selectable.cs (new partial)\n\nCreate `src/VisualRelay.Core/Configuration/BackendConfigGenerator.Selectable.cs` containing:\n\n- **`SelectableModels`** static `IReadOnlyDictionary<string, IReadOnlyList<string>>`:\n  - `frontier`: `glm-5.2`, `kimi-k2`, `deepseek-v4-pro`, `claude-opus-1m`, `gpt-5`, `hf-qwen3-coder-next`\n  - `balanced`: `deepseek-v4-pro`, `kimi-k2`, `deepseek-v4-flash`, `gpt-5`, `hf-qwen3-coder-next`, `claude-sonnet`\n  - `cheap`: `deepseek-v4-flash`, `deepseek-v4-pro`, `hf-qwen3-coder-next`, `gpt-5`\n  - `vision`: `hf-qwen3-vl-235b`, `hf-qwen3-vl-30b`, `kimi-k2`\n  - `claude`: `claude-opus-1m`, `claude-sonnet`\n  - `fallback`: `hf-qwen3-coder-next` (display-only, not editable)\n- **`ModelToRequiredKey`** extension dict: supplement `ModelToKey` with `(\"gpt-5\", \"OPENAI_API_KEY\")` so models not in any chain still resolve their env var.\n- Add to `TierConfigRow` (partial record):\n  - `IReadOnlyList<string> SelectableModels { get; init; } = Array.Empty<string>();`\n  - `bool IsEditable { get; init; } = true;`\n\n---\n\n### Step 3: BackendConfigGenerator.cs — override threading\n\nModify `src/VisualRelay.Core/Configuration/BackendConfigGenerator.cs`:\n\n1. Mark class `partial` (line 26: `public static class BackendConfigGenerator` → `public static partial class BackendConfigGenerator`).\n2. Mark `TierConfigRow` record `partial` (line 269).\n3. **`ResolveTiers`**: Add `IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? overrides = null` parameter. Logic:\n   - For each tier, if `overrides.TryGetValue(tier, out var ov)` and the override model's required key (from `ModelToKey` ∪ `ModelToRequiredKey`) ∈ `presentKeys` → alias = `ov`, fallbacks = all chain survivors (excluding `ov`), still terminating in `fallback` for non-`claude` tiers.\n   - Otherwise, auto-resolve as before.\n4. **`GetTierRows`**: Add `IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? overrides = null`. For each tier row, set `SelectableModels` = `SelectableModels[tier]`, `IsEditable` = tier != `\"fallback\"`.\n5. **`Generate`**: Add `IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? overrides = null`, pass to `ResolveTiers`.\n\n---\n\n### Step 4: RelayConfig — TierModelOverrides field\n\nModify `src/VisualRelay.Domain/RelayConfig.cs`:\n\n- Add to the sealed record: `public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? TierModelOverrides { get; init; }` (default null).\n- Add to `Defaults()` factory: `TierModelOverrides = null`.\n\n---\n\n### Step 5: RelayConfigLoader.TierModelOverrides.cs (new partial) + RelayConfigLoader.cs\n\n1. Mark `RelayConfigLoader` as `partial class` (line 14).\n2. Create `src/VisualRelay.Core/Configuration/RelayConfigLoader.TierModelOverrides.cs`:\n   - Static helper `TryParseTierModelOverrides(JsonElement el)` → `IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>?`: parse `tierModelOverrides` JSON object, validate each value against `BackendConfigGenerator.SelectableModels[tier]`, drop invalid entries, return null if empty/missing.\n3. In `RelayConfigLoader.TryLoadAsync`, after `tierProfiles` reading block (~line 105), add a 1-line call: if `root.TryGetProperty(\"tierModelOverrides\", out var tmoEl)`, call `TryParseTierModelOverrides(tmoEl)` and assign to `config = config with { TierModelOverrides = parsed }`.\n\n---\n\n### Step 6: RelayConfigWriter — UpsertTierModelOverrides\n\nModify `src/VisualRelay.Core/Init/RelayConfigWriter.cs`:\n\n- Add `public static void UpsertTierModelOverrides(string rootPath, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> overrides)` following the `UpsertCommitProofArtifacts` pattern: read `.relay/config.json`, parse as `JsonNode`, set `tierModelOverrides` object node, serialize back, write.\n\n---\n\n### Step 7: MainWindowViewModel.Keys.cs — TierModelRow + wiring\n\nModify `src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.Keys.cs`:\n\n1. Add nested `TierModelRow : ObservableObject` class:\n   - `[ObservableProperty] string _tier`, `_selectedModel`, `_providerName`\n   - `bool _keyPresent`, `bool _isEditable`\n   - `IReadOnlyList<string> _selectableModels`\n   - `partial void OnSelectedModelChanged(string value)` → calls a VM-level persist action (delegated).\n2. Change `LitTierRows` type to `ObservableCollection<TierModelRow>`.\n3. **`RefreshLitTiers`**:\n   - Load `RelayConfig` via `RelayConfigLoader.TryLoadAsync(RootPath)`, extract `TierModelOverrides`.\n   - Call `BackendConfigGenerator.GetTierRows(presentKeys, overrides: TierModelOverrides)`.\n   - Map each `TierConfigRow` → `TierModelRow`:\n     - `SelectedModel` = override from config (if present and key present) else auto-resolved `Model`.\n     - `SelectableModels` = row.SelectableModels, `IsEditable` = row.IsEditable.\n4. On `SelectedModel` change → detect diff → `RelayConfigWriter.UpsertTierModelOverrides(RootPath, newOverrides)` → call `RefreshLitTiers()` to reflect.\n\n---\n\n### Step 8: SettingsPanel.axaml — ComboBox per tier\n\nModify `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/SettingsPanel.axaml`:\n\n1. Change the Live Tiers `DataTemplate` `x:DataType` from `cfg:BackendConfigGenerator+TierConfigRow` to `vm:MainWindowViewModel+TierModelRow`.\n2. Replace the `TextBlock` bound to `Model` (line ~270) with:\n   ```xml\n   <ComboBox ItemsSource=\"{Binding SelectableModels}\"\n             SelectedItem=\"{Binding SelectedModel, Mode=TwoWay}\"\n             IsEnabled=\"{Binding IsEditable}\"\n             MinWidth=\"180\" />\n   ```\n3. Add a `TextBlock` below the Live Tiers header: \"Changes apply on the next backend start.\"\n4. Add `xmlns:vm=\"using:VisualRelay.App.ViewModels\"` if not already present.\n\n---\n\n### Step 9: BackendConfigStep — effect path\n\nModify `src/VisualRelay.Core/Execution/BackendConfigStep.cs`:\n\n- In `Generate` method (line ~80-91), after building `presentKeys` HashSet, load `RelayConfig` from `repoRoot` via `RelayConfigLoader.TryLoadAsync`, extract `TierModelOverrides`, pass to `BackendConfigGenerator.Generate(present, template, overrides: config.TierModelOverrides)`.\n\n---\n\n### Step 10: Tests — green\n\n**`RelayConfigLoaderTests.cs`** (5 new `[Fact]`s):\n- `TierModelOverrides_RoundTrip` — write config with `tierModelOverrides`, verify loader returns them.\n- `TierModelOverrides_InvalidEntryDropped` — entry not in `SelectableModels` is dropped.\n- `TierModelOverrides_MissingKey_DefaultsToNull` — no `tierModelOverrides` → `TierModelOverrides` is null.\n- `TierModelOverrides_EmptyObject_DefaultsToNull` — `{}` → null.\n- `TierModelOverrides_OtherKeysPreserved` — writer preserves existing keys.\n\n**`RelayConfigWriterTests.cs`** (2 new `[Fact]`s):\n- `UpsertTierModelOverrides_WritesAndSurvivesRoundTrip` — set overrides, reload, verify.\n- `UpsertTierModelOverrides_PreservesExistingKeys` — set overrides, verify other config keys intact.\n\n**`SettingsPanelUiTests.cs`** (3 new `[AvaloniaFact]`s):\n- `LiveTiers_HasComboBoxPerEditableTier` — each editable tier row has a ComboBox with correct options.\n- `FallbackTier_ComboBoxIsDisabled` — fallback row's combo `IsEnabled` is false.\n- `SelectingModel_WritesTierModelOverrides` — select a different model, verify `tierModelOverrides` in `.relay/config.json` survives reload.\n\n---\n\n### Step 11: Gate — `./visual-relay check`\n\nRun the full gate: format, build, all tests pass, file-size guards pass (no newly oversized file, baseline bumped correctly), screenshot comparison. Commit with a Conventional Commit message like `feat: per-tier LLM model selection in Live Tiers panel`.",
  "manifest": [

  "rationale": "Implemented per-tier LLM model selection in Settings → Live Tiers. Created BackendConfigGenerator.Selectable.cs with SelectableModels dictionary (6 tiers, ≤6 models each, all from 5 real providers). Added override-aware resolution: ResolveTiers/GetTierRows/Generate now accept optional IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string>? overrides — when an override model's key is present it wins as the alias with remaining survivors as fallback chain (still terminating in fallback for non-claude tiers); absent key → override silently ignored. Added RelayConfig.TierModelOverrides field, RelayConfigLoader.TryParseTierModelOverrides (validates against SelectableModels, drops invalid), and RelayConfigWriter.UpsertTierModelOverrides (read-modify-write). Extracted TierModelRow (ObservableObject with two-way SelectedModel) into MainWindowViewModel.TierModelRow.cs, updated LitTierRows binding and RefreshLitTiers to hydrate from config overrides. Replaced static Model TextBlock with ComboBox in SettingsPanel.axaml DataTemplate (ItemsSource=SelectableModels, SelectedItem=SelectedModel, IsEnabled=IsEditable), added 'Changes apply on next backend start' note. BackendConfigStep.ResolveAsync now loads overrides from .relay/config.json and passes through to Generate. All file sizes ≤300 lines. Baseline bumped 159→163 for the 4 new [Fact]s in BackendConfigGeneratorTests family (Override_WinsWhenKeyPresent, Override_IgnoredWhenKeyAbsent, SelectableModels_PerTierShapeAndCapped, GetTierRows_ExposesIsEditableAndSelectableModels). Core tests: 73 passed (24+18+16+15). Build clean, 0 warnings."
}

arguments.new_string:
<TextBlock Text="Live Tiers"
                       FontSize="9"
                       FontWeight="Bold"
                       Foreground="#7F8794"/>
            <TextBlock Text="Changes apply on the next backend start."
                       FontSize="8"
                       Foreground="#9AA3B1"
                       Margin="0,0,0,2"/>
            <ItemsControl x:Name="LitTierItems"
                          ItemsSource="{Binding LitTierRows}">
              <ItemsControl.ItemsPanel>
                <ItemsPanelTemplate>
                  <StackPanel Spacing="0"/>
                </ItemsPanelTemplate>
              </ItemsControl.ItemsPanel>
              <ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:MainWindowViewModel+TierModelRow">
                  <Grid ColumnDefinitions="Auto,Auto,Auto,Auto"
                        ColumnSpacing="6">
                    <!-- Green dot when key is present -->
                    <Ellipse Width="8" Height="8" Fill="#5AD47D"
                             VerticalAlignment="Center"
                             IsVisible="{Binding KeyPresent}"/>
                    <!-- Gray dot when key is missing -->
                    <Ellipse Width="8" Height="8" Fill="#4A5568"
                             VerticalAlignment="Center"
                             IsVisible="{Binding KeyPresent, Converter={x:Static controls:BoolNotConverter.Instance}}"/>
                    <!-- Tier name (bold) -->
                    <TextBlock Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Tier}"
                               FontWeight="SemiBold" Foreground="#E7ECF3"
                               VerticalAlignment="Center" FontSize="9"/>
                    <!-- Model ComboBox -->
                    <ComboBox Grid.Column="2"
                              ItemsSource="{Binding SelectableModels}"
                              SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedModel, Mode=TwoWay}"
                              IsEnabled="{Binding IsEditable}"
                              MinWidth="160"
                              FontSize="9"/>
                    <!-- Provider name (muted) -->
                    <TextBlock Grid.Column="3" Text="{Binding ProviderName}"
                               Foreground="#7F8794"
                               VerticalAlignment="Center" FontSize="9"/>
                  </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
              </ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
            </ItemsControl>

                  </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
              </ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
